title: cayratia roxburghii
intro: Cayratia roxburghii, also known as the Roxburgh's grape or wild grape, is a perennial climber belonging to the grape family (Vitaceae). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. These health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. Traditionally, Cayratia roxburghii has been used to prepare herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are often applied topically or consumed as teas to harness its medicinal properties.
